data.title: Kihealth secures $5M in seed funding for new diabetes diagnostic test, set for commercial launch.
data.description: A precision diagnostics company, Kihealth Inc., located in St. Augustine, Florida, has successfully concluded its seed funding round with a total of $5 million raised. This amount exceeded the initial target, showcasing high investor interest in the company's innovative methods for detecting diseases early. Kihealth's focus on metabolic health and disease prevention has garnered significant support, leading to the development of a minimally-invasive test that analyzes biomarkers linked to beta cell apoptosis. The funding secured will aid in the commercial launch of this pioneering diagnostic tool.

data.title: Report on Fraud Detection & Prevention in Banking Market 2025-2030 added to ResearchAndMarkets.com. Provides insights for stakeholders with comprehensive coverage.
data.description: In Dublin, a report titled "Fraud Detection & Prevention in Banking Market: 2025-2030" has been included in the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com. This report is a crucial asset for grasping the rapidly changing fraud environment, enabling stakeholders to develop effective strategies for the future. Providing thorough insights and broad coverage, this research suite is indispensable for navigating and strategizing in the quickly expanding landscape of fraud detection and prevention in the banking sector.

data.title: MotoPress launches Booking Calendar plugin for WordPress bookings, offering visual admin interface & dynamic pricing.
data.description: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida, on October 2, 2025, the WordPress booking specialists at MotoPress revealed their latest product, the Booking Calendar plugin. Tailored for rental and booking enterprises, this plugin offers a user-friendly admin interface, adaptable availability settings, and responsive pricing options to suit various rental needs.

data.title: Conagra beats low expectations with $2.63B in revenue, 5.8% decline. EPS drops 26.4% to 39 cents. Guidance: -1% to +1% sales, $1.70–$1.85 EPS, 11.0%–11.5% operating margins, tariffs may add 3% to costs. Bank of America predicts underperformance with $18 price.
data.description: Conagra Brands Inc. reported a 5.8% decline in revenue to $2.63 billion in the fiscal first quarter of 2026, with adjusted EPS dropping by 26.4% to 39 cents. Despite the decline, the results exceeded expectations due to trade expense timing and favorable mix effects. The company reaffirmed its outlook for organic net sales between -1% and +1%, operating margins of 11.0%-11.5%, and EPS of $1.70-$1.85, while cautioning that tariffs could add about 3% to costs, pushing overall inflation into the low-7% range. The high-stakes test in the back half of the year could be challenging due to tariffs and rising costs. Bank of America analyst Peter T. Galbo maintained an Underperform rating and a $18 price forecast, valuing it at 10x his calendar year 2026 EPS estimate.

data.title: General Dynamics to webcast 2025 third-quarter financial results conference call on October 24, starting at 9 a.m. EDT.
data.description: General Dynamics, a company listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol GD, is set to broadcast its third-quarter financial results conference call on Friday, October 24, at 9 a.m. Eastern Daylight Time. Interested parties can access the live webcast of the call on the company's website, www.gd.com. For those unable to tune in live, a replay of the conference call will also be accessible.

data.title: Study shows >99.9% PFAS removal efficiency during reactivation of spent drinking water carbon by Calgon Carbon.
data.description: The latest breakthrough in environmental technology has demonstrated an incredible efficiency rate of over 99.9% in destroying and removing PFAS contaminants. This milestone was reached through the full-scale thermal reactivation process of spent drinking water carbon under Calgon Carbon's standard operating protocols, utilizing OTM-45's rigorous calculation approach. The innovative method successfully eliminates PFAS from the spent carbon to undetectable levels, marking a significant advancement in environmental remediation efforts.

data.title: CME Group plans to launch 24/7 cryptocurrency futures trading in 2026 pending regulatory approval.
data.description: CME Group (NASDAQ: CME) has revealed plans to introduce 24/7 trading for cryptocurrency futures and options starting in early 2026, subject to regulatory clearance. The trading activity will take place on CME Globex, with a short weekend maintenance break. Transactions occurring between Friday evening and Sunday evening will be dated for the following business day, and all clearing and reporting will be managed accordingly. Visit Benzinga.com for detailed coverage.
